meta.description: SEA AI Upskilling Tracker — corporate skilling commitments across Malaysia, Indonesia, Thailand, Singapore
meta.notes: Analytical flags are editorial. Total headcount figures include overlapping and unverified targets. Update monthly.

entries.name: Bersama Malaysia
entries.source: MDEC / Microsoft ongoing program, cited in May 2024 announcement
entries.notes: Headline figure of 1.53M cited retrospectively in $2.2B announcement to establish track record. Depth of training unclear; broad digital literacy rather than AI-specific.

entries.name: AI Skilling — $2.2B commitment
entries.source: Microsoft May 2, 2024 announcement (Satya Nadella visit, KL)
entries.notes: Includes youth AI fluency, NGO workers, underserved communities. Framed as 3-year commitment. No accountability mechanism or outcome metrics in announcement.

entries.name: AI Odyssey (developer program)
entries.notes: Subset of 300K commitment. Target: developers earning Microsoft credentials as AI subject matter experts. More concrete than broad skilling; credential-based accountability.

entries.name: National AI Centre of Excellence
entries.source: Microsoft May 2024 announcement — Ministry of Digital partnership
entries.notes: Vague mandate: 'drive AI adoption across key industries, ensure AI governance.' Staffing, ownership, and output agenda not specified. Risk: Microsoft enterprise adoption unit dressed as sovereignty infrastructure.

entries.name: Google Cloud AI Skills — Malaysia
entries.notes: Near-identical headline figure to Microsoft commitment. Template language across SEA announcements.

entries.name: NVIDIA AI Skills Malaysia (MDEC MOU)
entries.source: MDEC-NVIDIA MOU, cited in YTL/ILMU context
entries.notes: Linked to YTL AI Labs ecosystem. Certification-focused; no population-level targets disclosed.

entries.name: AWS re/Start & Cloud Skills Malaysia
entries.notes: AWS re/Start program for unemployed/underemployed. Targeted rather than mass skilling. Job-placement linked — one of few programs with outcome accountability.

entries.name: AI Skilling — $1.7B commitment
entries.source: Microsoft April 2024 announcement (Satya Nadella visit, Jakarta)
entries.notes: Announced 2 weeks before Malaysia announcement. Larger target than Malaysia (840K vs 300K) but same structural template: broad skilling + developer program + govt partnership.

entries.name: SMK Coding Initiative (vocational)
entries.notes: Partnership with Ministry of Education targeting SMK (vocational high schools). More institutionally embedded than headline skilling programs.

entries.name: Google Career Certificates Indonesia
entries.notes: Workforce-oriented certification. Emphasis on employability in digital jobs rather than AI capability per se.

entries.name: GPU Merdeka / NVIDIA NeMo Skills
entries.source: Lintasarta / Indosat GPU Merdeka launch, Aug 2024
entries.notes: Developer-focused; linked to Sahabat AI LLM training. More technically substantive than broad population skilling — aimed at researchers and AI engineers.

entries.name: AWS Skills to Jobs — Indonesia
entries.notes: Certification pathways with employment focus. AWS re/Start program extended to Indonesia.

entries.name: AI Skilling — $1B+ commitment
entries.source: Microsoft March 31, 2026 Thailand announcement (Brad Smith visit)
entries.notes: Third major SEA investment announcement. Identical 'Technology / Trust / Talent' framework as Malaysia and Indonesia. Includes Ministry of Education and Ministry of Labor partnerships.
entries.flag: Template boilerplate — identical structure to MY/ID

entries.name: Microsoft Elevate for Educators / Changemakers
entries.notes: Education sector skilling. Partners include Gulf Development and CP Group — domestic conglomerates as ecosystem partners, not just passive recipients.

entries.name: Google Cloud Thailand Skilling
entries.source: Google Cloud Bangkok region launch, Jan 2024
entries.notes: Announced alongside Bangkok cloud region. '$40B economic value' projected over 5 years — economic impact framing rather than specific skilling targets.

entries.name: AWS Thailand Digital Skills
entries.notes: Part of AWS regional skills program. Thailand Digital Economy Promotion Agency (DEPA) partnership.

entries.name: Google AI Trailblazers SG
entries.notes: Enterprise-focused AI adoption program. Singapore programs typically more substantive / less template than SEA peers — higher baseline capability.

entries.name: Microsoft AI Singapore Partnership (IMDA)
entries.notes: Coordination with IMDA on AI governance and skilling. Singapore operates as regional anchor for Microsoft ASEAN capability hub.

entries.name: AWS re/Start Singapore
entries.notes: Longest-running AWS skilling program in region. Job-placement outcomes tracked — benchmark for what accountability looks like.

entries.name: NVIDIA AI Singapore Research / NUS Partnership
entries.notes: Research-oriented. Includes SEA-LION LLM project support. More substantive than headcount-based skilling commitments elsewhere.
